name: Crohn's Disease - IBD Medication Query (5-ASA + Biologics + Immunomodulators)
description: Tests ability to find Crohn's patients via IBD medications. Crohn's treatment uses three classes: 5-ASA (mesalamine, mostly UC but used in mild Crohn's), TNF inhibitors (infliximab, adalimumab — also used in RA), IL-12/23 inhibitor (ustekinumab — also used in psoriasis), and immunomodulators (azathioprine — also used in lupus, transplant). The Synthea module distributes patients across these classes.
prompt: Find patients prescribed inflammatory bowel disease medications used for Crohn's disease, including mesalamine, infliximab, adalimumab, ustekinumab, and azathioprine.

prompts.naive: Find patients on Crohn's disease medications.
prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed Crohn's disease therapies — 5-ASA aminosalicylates (mesalamine), TNF inhibitor biologics (infliximab, adalimumab), IL-12/23 inhibitor biologics (ustekinumab), and immunomodulators (azathioprine).
prompts.expert: I need MedicationRequest resources for any Crohn's disease therapy. Use these RxNorm ingredient codes: 52582 (mesalamine, 5-ASA), 191831 (infliximab, anti-TNF), 327361 (adalimumab, anti-TNF), 847083 (ustekinumab, IL-12/23 inhibitor), 1256 (azathioprine, immunomodulator). Combine in a single comma-separated code search. Note all have cross-indications — infliximab/adalimumab also for RA, ustekinumab also for psoriasis, azathioprine also for lupus/transplant.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with Crohn's IBD medication orders. Cross-indication makes this less specific than dx — but mesalamine + biologic combination is highly suggestive of IBD.
